blurb: Architectural 'bear's breeches' — huge glossy leaves and dramatic hooded white-and-purple flower spikes. Tender here (~zone 7); needs a sheltered spot and a hard mulch.
sun: Part shade–full sun (afternoon shade in hot sites)
spread: Clumping — spreads by deep roots; hard to remove once sited
seasons: Bold glossy foliage spring–fall; tall white-&-purple flower spikes early–mid summer
wildlife: Bees & bumblebees work the hooded flowers
toxic: Not considered toxic; sap and leaf-spines can irritate skin
